---
title: "AI for Coding"
subtitle: "Day 1: Setup overview"
date: "April 20, 2026"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [simple, custom.scss]
    include-after-body:
      - text: |
          <script>
          (function() {
            function toggleFooter() {
              var f = document.querySelector(".reveal > .footer");
              if (!f || typeof Reveal === "undefined") return;
              var i = Reveal.getIndices();
              f.style.display = (i.h === 0 && i.v === 0) ? "none" : "block";
            }
            if (typeof Reveal !== "undefined") {
              Reveal.on("slidechanged", toggleFooter);
              Reveal.on("ready", toggleFooter);
              if (Reveal.isReady()) toggleFooter();
            }
          })();
          </script>
    code-copy: false
    margin: 0.10
    html-math-method: mathjax
    slide-number: c/t
    background-transition: zoom
    preload-iframes: true
    scrollable: true
    transition: fade
    preview-links: auto
    logo: /assets/img/WBG-Prosperity Horizontal-Black.svg
    footer: '[worldbank.github.io/ai4coding](https://worldbank.github.io/ai4coding) © 2026 World Bank.'
    resources:
        - /assets/img/WBG-Prosperity Horizontal-Black.svg
---

## [Minimal]{.warning} and [**optimal**]{.fg} setup requirements {.small-slide}

To be able to reproduce examples and follow along with AI for coding in Stata,
you need to have a [minimal setup]{.warning} in place (or [optimal setup]{.fg} if possible). This includes:

- ![](/assets/img/positron.svg){style="width:35px;height:35px;vertical-align:middle;"} [**Software** —  Positron, Python, R, Stata]{.warning}, and [Quarto]{.fg}
- [Stata extension in Positron]{.warning}
- {{< fa brands github >}} [**GitHub account**]{.warning} on private email
- [Access to github.com/worldbank organization]{.fg}
- ![](/assets/img/github-copilot-icon.svg){style="width:25px;height:25px;vertical-align:middle;"} [**GitHub Copilot** activated]{.warning}
  - [WB organization subscription]{.fg} (recommended)
  - [or private subscription to the Pro plan]{.warning} (fall-back option)
- ![](/assets/img/positron-assistant-blue.svg){style="width:35px;height:35px;vertical-align:middle;"} [**Positron Assistant**]{.warning} — connected to Copilot and enabled in Positron
- [**Working folders**]{.warning} properly set up with data and code access.

If in doubt, [follow the detailed instructions in the prerequisites](/prerequisites/index.qmd){target="_blank"} and verify your setup.

## Software Setup {.small-slide}

[Follow instructions on software setup and verification]{.alert} in [prerequisites](/prerequisites/index.qmd).

- Thanks to our IT team, all required software is available for installation via
**Software Center** on WB laptops:
- **Stata 19+ MP**, **Positron** (latest), **R 4.5.3+**, **Python 3.13+**, **Quarto 1.9+**, and **Git 2.52+**
- Everything except Stata is **free and open source** - [no license required.]{.highlight}

If any problems arise: follow the [troubleshooting guide](/prerequisites/common-problems.qmd) or contact [ITHelp@worldbankgroup.org](mailto:ITHelp@worldbankgroup.org).


## {{< fa brands github >}} GitHub Account Setup {.small-slide}

**Why?** To use version control, collaborate with colleagues, and access GitHub Copilot AI features.

[1. Create account]{.fg}

- Sign up at [github.com/signup](https://github.com/signup)
- Use your [**personal email**]{.alert} (not WB email)

[2. Join WB organization]{.fg}

- Submit [eServices request](https://worldbankgroup.service-now.com/wbg/en/join-github-organization-account-request?id=wbg_sc_catalog&sys_id=910e1739db1a54903c5960ab13961912&table=sc_cat_item&searchTerm=github) to join [github.com/worldbank](https://github.com/worldbank)

- Accept invitation email within 7 days


## ![](/assets/img/github-copilot-icon.svg){style="width:40px;height:40px;vertical-align:middle;"} GitHub Copilot: WB vs. Private {.tiny-slide}

**Why?** To get AI code assistance in Positron and GitHub.

|  | Private Free | WB Organization | Private Copilot Pro | Private Copilot Pro+ |
|------|:------------:|:---------------:|:-------------------:|:--------------------:|
| **Cost** | Free | Free (WB-funded) | ~$10/mo | ~$39/mo |
| **Premium requests/mo** | 50 | 300 | 500 | 1,500 |
| **Advanced models** (Claude, GPT) | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| **Inline completions** | ⚠️ Old models | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| **Overage billing** | N/A | [Chargeback process](https://github.com/worldbank/ospo/blob/main/docs/copilot/premium-request-chargeback.md) | Credit card | Credit card |

::: {.callout-important}
You can only have [**one active plan**]{.alert}. WB access **automatically cancels and refunds** your private subscription. Switching back requires re-subscribing and is possible at the end of a month.
:::

## Premium Requests and Limits {.small-slide}

[What are premium requests?]{.fg}

Every chat/agent interaction is a **premium request** (with Claude, GPT-5, Gemini).

::::: {.columns}
:::: {.column width="50%"}
**Monthly included allowances**

| Plan | Req./month |
|-----------------------|:-----------:|
| WB org | 300 |
| Private Copilot Pro | 500 |
| Private Copilot Pro+ | 1,500 |


Check **usage** [GitHub Settings → Copilot](https://github.com/settings/copilot/features){target="_blank"}.

![](/assets/img/gh-max.png){width="80%"}

::::

:::: {.column width="50%"}
**Exhausted your premium requests?**

Set a **budget** (~$0.04/request):

- **WB plan →** [chargeback process](https://github.com/worldbank/ospo/blob/main/docs/copilot/premium-request-chargeback.md)
- **Private plan →** [GitHub Billing → Budgets](https://github.com/settings/billing/budgets)


::: {.small}
[When your usage reaches 100% capable AI models stop working.]{.warning}

- Basic inline completions still work.
:::

::::
:::::

## Access Levels at a Glance {.small-slide .center}

[For the course, it is recommended to have **WB Copilot** access + good prompting.]{.highlight}

[Fall-back: **Personal GH** + **Private Copilot Pro subscription**]{.note}

| Setup | Repos | Copilot |
|--------------|---------|-------------|
| No GitHub account | ✅ Public / ❌ Personal / ❌ WB | ❌ |
| [Personal account (free)]{.fg} | +✅Personal / ❌ WB | ⚠️ Only old LLMs |
| \+ Copilot Pro/Pro+ (private) | +✅Personal / ❌ WB | ✅Claude+ 300/1500 |
| \+ Premium req. budget (private) | +✅Personal / ❌ WB | ✅ + extra at 0.04\$/request (credit card) |
| [WB Org member]{.fg} | +✅ WB | ⚠️ No premium requests |
| \+ [or Private Copilot & budget]{.fg} | +✅ WB | ✅Claude+ 300/1500 + extra at 0.04\$/request (credit card) |
| \+ [or WB Copilot access]{.fg} | +✅ WB | ✅ 300 req/mo |
| \+ WB chargeback | +✅ WB | ✅ + extra at 0.04\$/request (WB chargeback) |

## GitHub @ World Bank is more than Copilot {.small-slide}

[**Why to use GitHub @ World Bank?**]{.fg}

- It is a modern standard of research and collaboration.

- GitHub integration improves your code, version control, and collaboration standards, leading to better quality, transparency, and reproducibility.

  - Request access to [github.com/worldbank](https://github.com/worldbank) on [eServices](https://worldbankgroup.service-now.com/wbg/en/join-github-organization-account-request?id=wbg_sc_catalog&sys_id=910e1739db1a54903c5960ab13961912&table=sc_cat_item&searchTerm=github)

- WB Open source [https://opensource.worldbank.org/](https://opensource.worldbank.org/) — builds open source communities and shares open data, software, and research to advance transparent, evidence-based progress toward the SDGs.
  - [make your code public](https://worldbankgroup.service-now.com/wbg/en/github-open-source-public-repository-request?id=wbg_sc_catalog&sys_id=63bb210e1b272610185ceb17b04bcbfa) as part of WB Open Source.

- [Viva Engage](https://engage.cloud.microsoft/main/groups/eyJfdHlwZSI6Ikdyb3VwIiwiaWQiOiIxNzA2ODQ1NDcwNzIifQ): stay connected with the community.

- Support: [github@worldbank.org](mailto:github@worldbank.org)

## ![](/assets/img/positron-assistant-blue.svg){style="width:40px;height:40px;vertical-align:middle;"} Positron Assistant {.small-slide}

The Positron Assistant connects GitHub Copilot to Positron, enabling AI features in the editor and chat.

::::: {.columns}
:::: {.column width="50%"}
**Enable**

1. In Positron: Settings (`Ctrl+,`) → search `positron.assistant.enable` → ☑️
2. Reload Positron

**Connect to Copilot**

1. `Ctrl+Shift+P` → *Configure Language Model Providers*
2. Select **GitHub Copilot**
3. Sign in → complete GitHub OAuth

::::

:::: {.column width="50%"}
**Use**

- Click chat icon in sidebar (or `Chat: Open Chat`)
- Type a question → press Enter
- Also provides inline code completions as you type

::: {.callout-tip}
You can also sign in via the **Accounts** icon (bottom-left of Activity Bar).
:::
::::
:::::

## Working folders setup and data (1/2)  {.small-slide}

::: {.callout-danger}
**Do not use OneDrive for coding with Positron during this course! (temporary)**

- Positron is new software for the WB.
- WB security policies **block Positron from writing text files** on OneDrive.
- [Do not save your code-in-progress in a OneDrive folder.]{.alert}
- New security policy will be implemented on **Friday, April 24** (spring meeting freeze).
:::

[**Step 1 — Create working folders**]{.fg}

- Create a folder `C:\WBG\ai\` on your local drive (not OneDrive).


## Working folders setup and data (2/2) {.small-slide}

[**Step 2 — Copy exercise templates and data**]{.fg} into `C:\WBG\ai\ai4coding-practice\` from:

- either [**OneDrive (WB only):** `ai4coding-examples`](https://worldbankgroup-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/g/personal/ebukin_worldbank_org/IgCXQVakSxEpT7VJFmKp_qtnAXx8RX9iemnwfGwITkEA3bE?e=U08qTX){target="_blank"}
- or [**GitHub repo worldbank/ai4coding-examples**](https://github.com/worldbank/ai4coding-examples){target="_blank"}

- Data is shared through **OneDrive (WB only):** [`ai4coding-data`](https://worldbankgroup-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/personal/ebukin_worldbank_org/Documents/AI/AI-course-2026-April/ai4coding-data?csf=1&web=1&e=ITG6Sk){target="_blank"}

- It is [read-only]{.alert}. You can sync it to your OneDrive and use it from there.

- If you encounter problems, copy the data to `C:\WBG\ai\ai4coding-data\`.

**Your local folder structure should look like:**

```
C:\WBG\ai\
├── ai4coding-practice\    ← exercise templates (you write here)
│   ├── ex01..\
│   ├── ex02..\
│   └── ...
└── ai4coding-data\        ← course data (optional)
```

## Example 0: Verify your setup

Quick [[Example 0]{.fg}](){target="_blank"} to verify your setup.

- Open `C:\WBG\ai\ai4coding-practice\ex00-positron-test` in Positron.
- Follow instructions in the README.md file to test your setup.

## Supporting materials and references {.small-slide}

| Need | Contact |
|------|---------|
| Software installation issues | [ITHelp@worldbankgroup.org](mailto:ITHelp@worldbankgroup.org) |
| GitHub / Copilot access | [github@worldbank.org](mailto:github@worldbank.org) |
| WB premium request chargeback | [github@worldbank.org](mailto:github@worldbank.org) + manager CC |
| Course materials | [worldbank.github.io/ai4coding](https://worldbank.github.io/ai4coding) |
| Course examples | [github.com/worldbank/ai4coding-examples](https://github.com/worldbank/ai4coding-examples) |
| Course data | **OneDrive (WB only):** [`ai4coding-data`](https://worldbankgroup-my.sharepoint.com/:f:/r/personal/ebukin_worldbank_org/Documents/AI/AI-course-2026-April/ai4coding-data?csf=1&web=1&e=ITG6Sk){target="_blank"} |

- Positron docs: [positron.posit.co](https://positron.posit.co/)
- GitHub Copilot docs: [docs.github.com/copilot](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot)
- WB GitHub overview: [github.com/worldbank/ospo](https://github.com/worldbank/ospo)

## Example 1: Explore Positron and Copilot (self-study) {.small-slide}

The goal of this exercise is to get familiar with Positron's AI assistance
features and how they can be used to write and execute Stata code. We go
through a typical workflow of creating a Stata do file, writing code with
AI assistance, and executing it in the console.

This exercise is pre-recorded for self-study (18 min), full details, suggested prompts, and instructions are in the [Self-Study > Example 1](/selfstudy/example-1.qmd){target="_blank"}.


## Example 2: Understanding and cleaning the data

**Documentation:**

- Course materials: [worldbank.github.io/ai4coding](https://worldbank.github.io/ai4coding)
